---
title: "Finance Dashboard"
format:
    dashboard:
        theme: spacelab
        orientation: rows
execute:
    echo: false
    warning: false
---

```{python}
#| results: hide
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import itables.options as opt
from itables import show

# load data
finance_data = pd.read_csv('finance.csv')

# data cleaning
finance_data.columns = finance_data.columns.str.strip()

# Rename common columns for ease of use
column_mapping = {
    'Totals.Capital outlay': 'total_capital',
    'Totals.Revenue': 'revenue',
    'Totals.Expenditure': 'expenditure',
    'Totals.General expenditure': 'total_gen_exp',
    'Totals.General revenue': 'total_gen_rev',
    'Totals.Insurance trust  revenue': 'total_ins_trust', 
    'Totals.Intergovernmental': 'total_intgov',
    'Totals.License tax': 'total_tax',
    'Details.Welfare.Welfare Institution Total Expenditure': 'welf_total_exp',
    'Details.Natural Resources.Parks.Parks Total Expenditure': 'park_exp',
    'Details.Transportation.Highways.Highways Total Expenditure': 'highway_exp',
    'Totals. Debt at end of fiscal year': 'total_debt',
    'Details.Insurance benefits and repayments': 'benef_rep',
    'Details.Interest on debt': 'int_debt',
    'Details.Interest on general debt': 'int_gen_debt',
    'Details.Miscellaneous general revenue': 'mis_rev',
    'Details.Other taxes': 'other_taxes',
    'Details.Police protection': 'pol_prot',
    'Details.Education.Education Total': 'education',
    'Details.Health.Health Total Expenditure': 'health',
    'Details.Welfare.Welfare Institution Total Expenditure': 'welfare',
    'Details.Transportation.Highways.Highways Total Expenditure': 'transportation'
}
    
finance_data = finance_data.rename(columns=column_mapping)

# remove US aggregate 
states_data = finance_data.query("State != 'UNITED STATES'").copy()

# calculate indicators
states_data['Budget Balance'] = states_data['revenue'] - states_data['expenditure']
states_data['Debt Ratio'] = (states_data['total_debt'] / states_data['revenue']) * 100
states_data['Education Share'] = (states_data['education'] / states_data['expenditure']) * 100

# Most Recent Year Data
latest_year = states_data['Year'].max()
recent_data = states_data[states_data['Year'] == latest_year].copy()

# state abbreviation for map
us_abbrev = {
    "ALABAMA": "AL", "ALASKA": "AK", "ARIZONA": "AZ", "ARKANSAS": "AR", "CALIFORNIA": "CA",
    "COLORADO": "CO", "CONNECTICUT": "CT", "DELAWARE": "DE", "FLORIDA": "FL", "GEORGIA": "GA",
    "HAWAII": "HI", "IDAHO": "ID", "ILLINOIS": "IL", "INDIANA": "IN", "IOWA": "IA",
    "KANSAS": "KS", "KENTUCKY": "KY", "LOUISIANA": "LA", "MAINE": "ME", "MARYLAND": "MD",
    "MASSACHUSETTS": "MA", "MICHIGAN": "MI", "MINNESOTA": "MN", "MISSISSIPPI": "MS", "MISSOURI": "MO",
    "MONTANA": "MT", "NEBRASKA": "NE", "NEVADA": "NV", "NEW HAMPSHIRE": "NH", "NEW JERSEY": "NJ",
    "NEW MEXICO": "NM", "NEW YORK": "NY", "NORTH CAROLINA": "NC", "NORTH DAKOTA": "ND", "OHIO": "OH",
    "OKLAHOMA": "OK", "OREGON": "OR", "PENNSYLVANIA": "PA", "RHODE ISLAND": "RI", "SOUTH CAROLINA": "SC",
    "SOUTH DAKOTA": "SD", "TENNESSEE": "TN", "TEXAS": "TX", "UTAH": "UT", "VERMONT": "VT",
    "VIRGINIA": "VA", "WASHINGTON": "WA", "WEST VIRGINIA": "WV", "WISCONSIN": "WI", "WYOMING": "WY",
    "DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA": "DC"
}
recent_data['code'] = recent_data['State'].map(us_abbrev)

# KPI Calcs
avg_rev = recent_data['revenue'].mean()
avg_exp = recent_data['expenditure'].mean()
avg_debt_ratio = recent_data['Debt Ratio'].mean()

top_exp = recent_data.nlargest(5, 'expenditure')
exp_bar= px.bar(top_exp, x='State', y='expenditure', color='expenditure', 
       color_continuous_scale='Viridis', labels={'expenditure': 'Total Expenditure ($)'}, title= "Highest State Expenditure",
       template='simple_white')

map= px.choropleth(recent_data, locations='code', locationmode='USA-states', color='expenditure',
              scope='usa', color_continuous_scale='Viridis', title="Total Expenditure by State (2019)")

sector_data = top_exp.melt(id_vars='State', value_vars=['education', 'health', 'welfare', 'transportation'], var_name='sector', value_name='spending')

high_bar = px.bar(sector_data, x='State', y='spending', color='sector', barmode='stack', template='simple_white')

# filtering for trend analysis
trend_data = states_data[states_data['State'] == 'CALIFORNIA'].sort_values('Year')

trend_line = px.line(trend_data, x='Year', y=['revenue', 'expenditure'], 
        labels={'value': 'Amount ($)', 'variable': 'Category'},
        title="California Fiscal Trend (1992-2019)", template='simple_white')

scatter_plot = px.scatter(recent_data, x='revenue', y='total_debt', size='expenditure', color='State',
           hover_name='State', title="Relationship between State Revenue and Debt",
           labels={'revenue': 'Total Revenue ($)', 'total_debt': 'Total Debt ($)'},
           template='simple_white')
```

# Home
## Row {height="20%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Average State Revenue (2019)"
dict(icon="cash-stack", color="primary", value=f"${avg_rev/1e6:.1f}B")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Average State Expenditure (2019)"
dict(icon="cart-check", color="info", value=f"${avg_exp/1e6:.1f}B")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Average Debt Ratio (2019)"
dict(icon="percent", color="danger", value=f"{avg_debt_ratio:.1f}%")
```

## Row {height="60%"}

```{python}
exp_bar
```

```{python}
map
```

# Trend
## Row {height="50%"}

```{python}
trend_line
```

## Row {height="50%"}

```{python}
scatter_plot
```

# Data Source

```{python}
opt.features = ["paging", "ordering", "info", "buttons"]
table_data = states_data[['State', 'Year', 'revenue', 'expenditure', 'total_debt', 'Budget Balance', 'Debt Ratio']].copy()

show(table_data, caption="U.S. State Finance Historical Data", 
     classes="display nowrap compact", buttons=["csv", "excel"])
```
